creator: utisoft@gmail.com
text:
As requested in ports/152823 here is a port for libprojectm. Following
shortly will be a PR for projectm-libvisual. The original requests are at [1] and [2], I announced and requested testers at [3].

	There is a PR for libvisual04 to stop it conflicting with libvisual02 at ports/154530 ; this would be a nice one to fix before this! This port works fine without the fix, but only as long as libvisual02 isn't installed.

	Anyone who uses a libvisual compatible music player might be
interested in this port; according to Wikipedia these:

   * Amarok
   * XMMS2
   * Beep Media Player
   * BMPx
   * Audacious
   * LiVES Video Editing System
   * Rhythmbox

projectM is a Free rewrite of Milkdrop, which is a visualiser for 'Winamp'.

Submitted by: Chris Rees (utisoft@gmail.com)

[1] http://forums.freebsd.org/showthread.php?t=12710
[2] http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=ports/152823
[3] http://www.mail-archive.com/freebsd-ports@freebsd.org/msg31912.html

Fix: I've seen some committers saying that they prefer diffs to /nonexistant, that's posted
at http://www.bayofrum.net/~chris/patches/libprojectm.diff.

Tested:	http://tinderbox.bayofrum.net/index.php?action=describe_port&id=256



-- 
This message has been scanned for viruses and
dangerous content by MailScanner, and is
believed to be clean.--aw0gqyPmzALbaZrMLdYFNqkRIiSiAcCydaurvYFRlLUynBYX
Content-Type: text/plain; name="libprojectm.shar"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit
Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="libprojectm.shar"

# This is a shell archive.  Save it in a file, remove anything before
# this line, and then unpack it by entering "sh file".  Note, it may
# create directories; files and directories will be owned by you and
# have default permissions.
#
# This archive contains:
#
#	graphics/libprojectm
#	graphics/libprojectm/Makefile
#	graphics/libprojectm/distinfo
#	graphics/libprojectm/pkg-descr
#	graphics/libprojectm/pkg-plist
#
echo c - graphics/libprojectm
mkdir -p graphics/libprojectm > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - graphics/libprojectm/Makefile
sed 's/^X//' >graphics/libprojectm/Makefile << '6ac1875501155737969361e738c8e2fd'
X# New ports collection Makefile for:	libprojectM
X# Date created:	 	8 December 2010
X# Whom:			utisoft@gmail.com
X#
X# $FreeBSD$
X#
X
XPORTNAME=	libprojectM
XPORTVERSION=	2.0.1
XCATEGORIES=	graphics audio
XMASTER_SITES=	SF/${PORTNAME:L:S/lib//}/${PORTVERSION}/
XDISTNAME=	${PORTNAME:S/lib//}-${DISTVERSION}-Source
X
XMAINTAINER=	utisoft@gmail.com
XCOMMENT=	An awesome music visualiser
X
XLICENSE=	LGPL21
X
XLIB_DEPENDS=	ftgl:${PORTSDIR}/graphics/ftgl \
X		GLEW:${PORTSDIR}/graphics/glew \
X		freetype:${PORTSDIR}/print/freetype2
X
XUSE_CMAKE=	yes
XCFLAGS+=	-I${LOCALBASE}/include -L${LOCALBASE}/lib
XUSE_LDCONFIG=	yes
XUSE_PKGCONFIG=	yes
X
Xpost-patch:
X# Install config.inp.sample instead of overwriting user's file.
X# Needs to be specific enough not to rename config.inp.in too!
X	@${REINPLACE_CMD} -e 's/config.inp\([\" ]\)/config.inp.sample\1/' \
X			-e 's#/pkgconfig#data/pkgconfig#' \
X		${WRKSRC}/CMakeLists.txt
X	@${REINPLACE_CMD} -e 's#/usr/X11R6#${LOCALBASE}#' \
X		${WRKSRC}/cmake/FindFreetype2.cmake
X
Xpost-install:
X	@if [ ! -f ${PREFIX}/share/projectM/config.inp ]; then \
X		${CP} -p ${PREFIX}/share/projectM/config.inp.sample \
X			${PREFIX}/share/projectM/config.inp ; \
X	fi
X
X.include <bsd.port.mk>
6ac1875501155737969361e738c8e2fd
echo x - graphics/libprojectm/distinfo
sed 's/^X//' >graphics/libprojectm/distinfo << '16d5421fa24fc7186a3c10d1ea4e1897'
XSHA256 (projectM-2.0.1-Source.tar.gz) = c305182dfc8b7f87b82a146af6cf50a982e7c9c647877a0559dfef4b11dacb93
XSIZE (projectM-2.0.1-Source.tar.gz) = 865784

XprojectM is an awesome music visualizer. There is nothing better in the world
Xof Unix. projectM's greatness comes from the hard work of the community. Users
Xlike you can create presets that connect music with incredible visuals. Try it!
X
XWWW: http://projectm.sourceforge.net/
